title: Automated Client Reporting for Web Design Agencies
metaDescription: Monthly site care and retainer reports that draft themselves from live analytics, uptime, and project data. The PM reviews and sends. Done-for-you, not a template.
excerpt: Monthly retainer and site care reports usually take PM time on the first of every month. They do not have to. Analytics, uptime, and project data are live; the report should draft itself.

sections.intro:
If your agency runs site care or monthly retainers, you ship a report on the first of every month per client. Pageview numbers, uptime, security updates, performance wins, work shipped, hours used. Most agencies build that by hand in a Notion doc or PDF. It is not hard work, but it is half a day every month.

The data is live in analytics, uptime monitors, and the project tool. The report should draft itself, in your studio's voice, on the first. The PM reviews and sends.

This is the Cadence layer of an AI Operating System.
sections.fourCFit:
Reporting is a Cadence capability: schedule-driven, no human prompt needed. It depends on:

  • Connections: analytics (GA4, Plausible, Fathom), uptime monitor, your project tool (ClickUp, Asana, Notion), and your time tracker.
  • Context: your studio voice and the standard sections a retainer report has at your shop.
  • Capabilities: the draft itself, formatted to your template.

See it in context at AI Operating System for Web Design Agencies.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
• Report drafts on the first of each month per retainer client, automatically.
  • Pulls live numbers: traffic, uptime, performance metrics, security updates applied, work shipped, hours used vs allocated.
  • Written in your studio voice, references last month's numbers, flags trends worth talking about.
  • Format matches your existing template (PDF, Notion, Google Doc, email body).
  • PM reviews, edits if needed, sends. Time per report drops from hours to under 15 minutes.
sections.howWeInstall:
Three phases:

  1. Audit, free, about 10 minutes. Run the audit.
  2. Install, inside 10 business days. We connect your analytics, uptime, project tool, and time tracker, capture your studio voice from past reports, and ship the first draft.
  3. Operate, ongoing. We watch and extend it as part of your plan. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
• Time per monthly report: from hours to under 15 minutes.
  • Coverage: 100 percent of retainer clients reported on the first, every month.
  • Consistency: every client gets the same depth, regardless of which PM owns the account.

Standing guarantee on the wider AIOS engagement: recover 40 or more hours a month across the team, or we keep working free until you do.
sections.faq:
What about clients with different retainer tiers?

Per-client tiers and sections are part of the setup. Each client gets the right depth for what they pay for, automatically.

What if a data source breaks?

We monitor every connection. If a source goes down, the draft flags it and pings the PM. No silent failures.

Who owns the system?

You do. Every prompt and connection lives in your accounts.
